name: Max current available from +5V GPIO Pin
snippet: Nov 18, 2021 — The current limit on the 5V pin is limited by the copper tracks linking the the pin to the 5V usb-c port, so if you limit it to around 1-1.5A you should be ok.

max of 5v pin: 5v, 750mA. max of other GPIO pins: 3.3v, 50mA. GPIO in: 3.3v, 16mA.

name: 5v Power at Raspberry Pi GPIO Pinout
snippet:
The 5v power pins are connected directly to the Pi's power input and will capably provide the full supply current of your mains adaptor.
With a decent power supply, such as the official Pi adaptor, you can expect to pull about 1.5A. This varies by Pi model and adapter used. Missing: limit | Show results with: limit

name: GPIO current limits - Raspberry Pi
snippet: Sep 4, 2019 — The 50mA is referring to all the GPIO's added up in total shouldn't draw more than 50mA and the 3mA is per pin.

name: How much current can I put into my RPi?
snippet: Maximum permitted current draw from the 5 V pin is the USB input current (usually 1 A) minus any current draw from the rest of the board. Model A: 1000 mA - 500 ...

name: 5v pin max current draw - Raspberry Pi Forums
snippet: The 4B uses a maximum of 1A25 under stress, and the USB peripherals are limited to 1A2. If you have the official 5V1 3A PSU, that leaves 3-1.25-1.2=0.55 (550mA

name: Voltage and Amperage Specs for GPIO Pins - Raspberry Pi Forums
snippet: The GPIO total output current limit is 100mA for the newer models (40 pin) and 50mA for the older models (26 pin), with a limit of 16mA per GPIO pin.

name: Comprehensive Guide to the Pin Diagram of Raspberry Pi 5: Understanding GPIO Pins and Their Functions - SunFounder
